user_message: I have this email thread from Contoso Ltd that needs identify the blockers.

input_artifacts.content:
Meeting: territory planning Discussion - Contoso Ltd
Date: 2024-02-07
Duration: 45 minutes
Attendees: Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd), Marcus Williams (Microsoft)

[00:02:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Good morning. I wanted to follow up on territory planning from our last session.

[00:06:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around partner engagement.

[00:11:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about integration complexity. Can we address those?

[00:15:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Our director reviewed this and has concerns about vendor dependencies. Can we address those?

[00:18:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is partner engagement.

[00:20:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is technical validation.

[00:24:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): Our architect reviewed this and has concerns about resource allocation. Can we address those?

[00:28:00] Angela Doyle (Contoso Ltd): On the territory planning front, we've made progress but there are still blockers around performance requirements.

[00:33:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): The timeline for partner engagement is aggressive. We need to validate the assumptions.

[00:35:00] Marcus Williams (Microsoft): I agree with that assessment. From our side, the main concern is resource allocation.

handoffs.condition: ECIF workscope or partner engagement warranted

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF49-N trap (No native 'workshop-capture' primitive -- synthesis spans 4 skills); produces complete Workshop recap, ECIF workscope draft (if warranted), partner plan, follow-up items with owners, mile
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF49-N failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF49-N failure mode (No native 'workshop-capture' primitive -- synthesis spans 4 skills)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct territory planning decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF49-N -- No native 'workshop-capture' primitive -- synthesis spans 4 skills
Produce structural verdict on whether current routing adequately serves this scenario
